title: Ctenopteris flexuosa
intro: Ctenopteris flexuosa, also known as the flexible tree fern, is a species of tree fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris flexuosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ctenopteris flexuosa is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a tincture or extract, to support its use in traditional remedies for pain relief and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Ctenopteris flexuosa is a species of tree fern, and its classification is as follows: It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. The genus Ctenopteris is also part of the family Dryopteridaceae. This species is native to New Zealand and is often found in forests and woodlands. The classification of Ctenopteris flexuosa is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Dryopteridaceae, genus Ctenopteris, and species C. flexuosa.
